name: StarDist (Star-Convex Cell/Nucleus Segmentation)
pricing_notes: Open source (BSD-3-Clause); Martin Weigert / Uwe Schmidt / Max Planck

auth.notes: Local library; no auth required; requires TensorFlow

performance.notes: GPU-accelerated; 2D inference fast; 3D volumetric data is slow

agent_readiness.notes: Star-convex object segmentation for fluorescence microscopy (2D+3D). from stardist.models import StarDist2D. model = StarDist2D.from_pretrained('2D_versatile_fluo'). labels, details = model.predict_instances(img_normalized). Pretrained models: 2D_versatile_fluo, 2D_versatile_he, 3D_demo. normalize: from csbdeep.utils import normalize. img_norm = normalize(img, 1, 99.8). 3D: from stardist.models import StarDist3D. Custom training: model = StarDist2D(None, name='custom', basedir='models'). model.train(X_train, Y_train). Napari plugin: napari-stardist. Works with skimage, TiffFile images. Returns label array with unique cell IDs.

gotchas: TensorFlow dependency — conflicts with PyTorch-heavy environments
Input must be normalized: normalize(img, 1, 99.8) is critical for good results
Assumes star-convex shapes — doesn't work well for elongated/irregular cells
3D segmentation is very slow without GPU
Labels are not sorted by area or position — post-process as needed
best_when: Fluorescence microscopy nuclei segmentation (DAPI, Hoechst channels)
H&E pathology cell segmentation with 2D_versatile_he model
When Cellpose is too slow or produces poor results on round nuclei
Automated cell counting and morphology quantification
avoid_when: Non-star-convex objects (neurites, bacteria chains — use Cellpose or SAM)
When PyTorch-only environment is needed (use Cellpose)
summary: StarDist segments star-convex nuclei/cells in fluorescence microscopy. StarDist2D.from_pretrained('2D_versatile_fluo').predict_instances(normalize(img)). Returns label array. Pretrained: 2D_versatile_fluo, 2D_versatile_he, 3D_demo. Custom training supported. Napari plugin. BSD-3 by Weigert/Schmidt/Max Planck. Best for round nuclei segmentation. Normalization critical. TF dependency. Not for elongated/irregular cells (use Cellpose).
